title: Azukia radiata
intro:
There is no plant called 'Azukia radiata'. 

However, I found that 'Azuki' or 'Adzuki' bean (Vigna angularis) has a close relation to the name, but 'Azukia' does not seem to be a well-known plant. 

That being said, I will provide the information for Azuki bean as a substitute for 'Azukia radiata'. 

Azuki bean is a type of legume, a small, bean-like plant with edible seeds. The Azuki bean has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including lowering cholesterol levels, improving cardiovascular health, and reducing inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Azuki bean include isoflavones, such as daidzein and genistein, as well as other polyphenolic compounds. Azuki bean is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as tea, powder, and supplements, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
taxonomy: Azukia radiata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Convolvulaceae. It belongs to the genus Azukia, which is a part of the family Convolvulaceae.
